channel.title: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN
channel.description: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN RSS

item.title: Houses ‘depreciated’ 17% in 2025
item.description:
Housing units traded throughout 2025 depreciated by 17%, compared to the previous year, with an average price of 70,935 patacas per square meter, according to data from the Statistics and Census Services Department, which represents the eighth consecutive drop and the biggest loss in value in recent years. 135 fewer houses were also transacted, compared to 2024, taking into account that, last year, 3,245 residential properties were sold, representing an annual drop of 4%
PEDRO MILHEIRÃO Last year, the number of housing units transacted fell to a total of 3,245, 135 fewer (-4%) than in 2024, official data indicates. The value of transacted houses registered an even greater drop, if we consider the evolution of the average price per useful area. Between 2024 and 2025, the average price per square meter of transacted houses was 70,935 patacas, corresponding to a devaluation of 16.7%, the eighth consecutive drop and the biggest drop in recent years. Analyzing by region, in Taipa, homes were sold for an average price of 72,971 patacas per square meter last year, 22.3% below the average value for 2024. Coloane recorded the second biggest annual drop (-18.2%), with an average price of 81.5 thousand patacas per square meter. In the Peninsula, the decline was lighter compared to the islands, reaching 68,894 patacas, 12.5% ​​below the previous year. In quarterly terms, homes transacted in the last three months of 2025 depreciated by 8.3%, compared to the period from July to August, ending the month of December with an average price of 68,048 patacas. Contrary to the annual trend, in the Peninsula, the value of houses sold registered the biggest quarterly drop, compared to the islands, with an average price of 64,965 patacas per square meter. In Taipa, this indicator stood at 69,650 patacas, while in Coloane it stood at 83,961 patacas, translating into decreases of 0.7% and 3.6%, respectively, on the same comparative basis. The Statistics and Census Services Directorate (DSEC) highlighted that “the average price of housing units in buildings with five or fewer years of construction fell by 10.4%”, while “that of buildings with six to 10 years old and buildings with more than 30 years of construction grew by 3.2% and 0.9%, respectively”. “The average price per square meter of autonomous housing units in buildings under construction decreased by 0.2%, in quarterly terms”, said DSEC. NAPE and the Praia Grande Bay Landfill area recorded the highest average value, of 88,490 patacas per useful area, with houses already built being sold for an average price of 74,868 patacas per square meter, while the value of those still under construction was 114,441 patacas. In Horta e Costa and Ouvidor Arriaga, housing units under construction cost, on average, 96,874 patacas per square meter. In Coloane, housing units were sold for an average price of 83,961 patacas, all of which were already built. In the last quarter, houses measuring less than 50 square meters were sold for an average price of 70,733 patacas per square meter, with units of this size being worth, on average, 68,372 and 62,222 patacas in the Peninsula and Taipa, respectively. Similarly, the largest residential units in the Peninsula were also more expensive than those in Taipa, considering that in the former they cost an average of 73,608 patacas per square meter, while in Taipa houses over 150 square meters cost 58,815 patacas per square meter. By useful area, houses between 100 and 150 square meters in Taipa were those that registered the highest average price, of 89,306 patacas. By the end of the fourth quarter, there were 5,993 residential units in the design phase, 1,620 under construction and 51 completed. Of the houses in the design phase, 3,870 are located in the Peninsula, 863 in Taipa and 1,260 in Coloane. To be built, 1,331 relate to the Peninsula, 286 to Taipa and only three to Coloane. Those that have been completed are all located on the Peninsula. In 2025, there were 1,068 housing units with a use license already issued and 383 with execution authorization granted, with 382 of the latter being located on the Peninsula.

item.title: “Drivers’ attitude” motivated 78% of complaints against taxis
item.description:
In 2025, the Directorate of Services for Traffic Affairs received 1,224 complaints against taxi drivers, an annual increase of 11%. The data reveals that the majority, 78%, were motivated by the “attitude of drivers in the service”
Catarina Pereira
Over the past year, complaints against taxi drivers that reached the Directorate of Services for Traffic Affairs (DSAT) totaled 1,224, an increase of around 11% year-on-year. The majority of complaints, 952 or 77.8% of the total, were aimed at the “attitude of drivers on duty”. According to the organization's statistics, 144 concerned “suggestions” and 116 “other” complaints, which are not specified. DSAT also received 12 complaints about taxi equipment. Despite thousands of complaints, only 163 cases of payment of fines were recorded, referring to taxi drivers who violated the provisions of the Regulation for the Transport of Passengers in Light Cars for Hire or Taxis and the Legal Regime for the Transport of Passengers in Light Cars for Hire, whose cases were initiated by DSAT. In total, the amount paid throughout 2025 by the offending taxi drivers was 177 thousand patacas. DSAT emphasizes that the number of cases and the amount paid “correspond to confirmed infractions and in which taxi drivers have already paid the fines in that quarter”. Regarding the number of unpaid fines and the amount involved, DSAT does not reveal information. It should be remembered that, according to data from the Public Security Police Corps, irregularities committed by taxi drivers more than doubled last year, with 1,898 cases recorded, a volume that reflects an increase of 118.16% compared to 2024. Of these cases, 605 were handed over to DSAT. Still taking into account the corporation's data, the vast majority of cases were related to taxi drivers who did not wait in order of arrival for customers in the territory's plazas. Specifically, 1,110 situations of this type were recorded, that is, 71% of the total, having registered an annual increase of 306.59%. There were also 428 cases of refusal to transport, 56.2% more than a year before, and 110 cases of obvious route deviation, with this type of infractions rising by 2.8%. With regard to abusive charging, 43 cases were detected, an annual increase of 72%. On the other hand, DSAT data reveals that 14 taxi driver identification cards were canceled last year. Cancellation occurs when, within a period of five years, four infractions defined in the regulations are committed. At the end of last year, there were 7,749 professionals with a valid taxi driver identification card. As for the number of taxis in circulation on Macau's roads, there were 1,258 normal (black) taxis and 200 special taxis. The specials included 173 normal-sized taxis, 10 taxis with lift seats and seven accessible taxis. According to DSAT, last year overall, the organization received more than 63,000 complaints and inquiries from the public, many of which were related to the Delta Bridge, buses, taxis, among others.

item.title: Dismissed employees can appeal within the scope of national security
item.description:
After the second meeting of the AL Commission that is analyzing the proposed law on the State Security Defense Commission, it became known that a public employee who is dismissed after being considered a risk to national security can appeal
A civil servant who is fired after being deemed by the State Security Defense Commission (CDSE) to be a risk to China's national security will be able to appeal, a parliamentary aide said. Leong Sun Iok, the chairman of the Legislative Assembly (AL) committee that is analyzing the proposed law on the CDSE regime, recalled yesterday that there is no possible appeal for the decisions and opinions of this body. However, the deputy added that, in a meeting held yesterday, the Secretary for Security, Chan Tsz King, agreed to amend the proposal, which was generally approved unanimously on 10 February. “Whether or not decisions taken by other entities based on the [CDSE] opinion are unimpeachable or appealable depends on the specific provisions of each law in question,” explained Leong, including the Public Administration Workers Statute. When asked by Lusa whether an employee who had been disciplined or dismissed by the public service to which he belongs, after receiving a negative opinion from the CDSE, could appeal or not, the deputy did not respond. But Vu Ka Vai, an AL legal advisor, confirmed to Lusa that the statute allows public service workers to appeal disciplinary decisions, including to the courts. On the other hand, Vu downplayed the possible role of the CDSE: “I don't see where the Commission can intervene in a disciplinary process, unless it is a question of loyalty or not defending the Macao SAR”. From November onwards, all civil servants, including those of Portuguese nationality, must swear an oath of loyalty to China and the Macao SAR, with refusal automatically implying cancellation of appointment or dismissal. According to official data, at the end of 2025 the public service had 33,856 workers, 325 fewer than in 2024. The statistics do not reveal how many have Portuguese nationality, mentioning only that 226 were born in Portugal. On January 1, the Government leader, Sam Hou Fai, revealed that, by the end of 2025, all public servants had already taken the oath of loyalty, provided for in a review approved in December 2024. The review provides that public servants can be fired if they carry out “acts contrary” to their oath, particularly due to private conversations or comments published on social media. Also yesterday, Vu Ka Vai told Lusa that, in practice, the CDSE will have the final say on whether or not trials linked to national security will be held behind closed doors. Asked whether a judge could decide to hold an open trial even if the CDSE issues an opinion confirming that it involves “State security interests”, the advisor replied: “There cannot be this type of situation”.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: War in the Middle East affects Portuguese travel to Macau
item.description:
epa12786243 Passengers queue for assistance from Qatar Airways staff after their flight was canceled at Ngurah Rai International Airport in Bali, Indonesia, 01 March 2026. At least four flights from Bali to Doha and Dubai have been canceled amid US and Israeli strikes on Iran. EPA/MADE NAGI
More than 110 Portuguese people saw their trips to Macau affected by the closure of airports in the Middle East due to the conflict in the region, the Macau Tourist Industry Association told Lusa yesterday. “A group of around 80 people, who were traveling from Portugal to Macau with a stopover in the Middle East, were supposed to arrive yesterday [Sunday], but the trip was postponed”, said the president of the association, Wu Keng Kuong, adding that “there are groups of more than 30 people from Portugal who have also postponed their trip to Macau for the near future”. Wu explained that the Middle East is a crucial hub connecting Europe and Hong Kong and Macau, “especially for routes to and from Portugal”, most of which stop in Doha, Qatar or Dubai, in the United Arab Emirates. “Currently, the suspension of flights at some of the region’s main airports is disrupting the movement of passengers”, said the official, but, despite the disruptions, the overall impact of the situation in the Middle East on Macau’s tourism sector “continues to be low”, he added. The Macau Tourism Board (DST) has updated the travel warning for Iran and Israel to the highest level, “extreme threat”, recommending that Macao SAR residents not travel to these countries at this time. DST has so far received seven requests for information related to the suspension of flights and matters related to people stranded in Dubai, Abu Dhabi and Bahrain. “According to the information obtained, at this moment, there are no tour groups from Macau traveling to Europe via the Middle East or to the Middle East,” added DST, in information published on its website. Israel and the US launched a military attack against Iran on Saturday, to allegedly “eliminate imminent threats from the Iranian regime”, and Tehran responded with missiles and drones against US bases in the region and Israeli targets. The American President, Donald Trump, stated that the operation aims to “eliminate imminent threats” from Iran and the Israeli Prime Minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, justified the joint action against what he classified as an “existential threat”. Iran has already confirmed the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the country's supreme leader since 1989, and declared a 40-day mourning period. According to the Iranian Red Cross, at least 200 deaths and around 750 injuries were recorded. Portugal, France, Germany and the United Kingdom condemned Iranian attacks on neighboring countries. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs recommended that Portuguese people in the Middle East region comply with the recommendations of local authorities, stay at home, and, in case of emergency, contact embassies or consulates.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: USA, Israel and Iran maintain attacks and conflict extends to Lebanon
item.description:
The war in the Middle East expanded yesterday to Lebanon, with Israeli attacks in retaliation for firing by the pro-Iranian Islamist movement Hezbollah, on the third day of large-scale bombings by the US and Israel against Iran.
The Israeli Army carried out simultaneous attacks “with hundreds of planes” in Iran and Lebanon, and warned that Hezbollah will pay “dearly” for opening fire on Israel. Tel Aviv announced that it intercepted a “projectile” launched from Lebanon and that others fell in uninhabited areas, without causing any casualties. Hezbollah committed to launching “a salvo of missiles” and drones against Israel to avenge the death of Iranian supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in the operation by American and Israeli forces. This was Hezbollah's first attack on Israel since the November 2024 ceasefire, which ended more than a year of war between the parties. Lebanese authorities announced a death toll of at least 31 and around 150 injured. In Iran, the local Red Crescent indicated that at least 555 people have died since the start of the conflict, which interrupted negotiations with Washington over Tehran's nuclear program. To avenge the death of Khamenei and several leaders of the Islamic Republic, Tehran has launched missiles against several countries in the region, including several that host American bases. Residents of Doha, Abu Dhabi and Dubai woke up once again to the sound of explosions, on a day when a column of smoke surrounded the US embassy in Kuwait. US Central Command reported that three US fighter jets were shot down by Kuwaiti air defense, “due to an apparent friendly fire incident”. However, “all six crew members ejected safely, were rescued” and their health status is “stable”, he assured. Iran also attacked Israel, where at least nine people died on Sunday. According to the Revolutionary Guard, Iran's ideological army, one of the targets of the attacks was the office of the Israeli Prime Minister, Benjamin Netanyahu. An AFP photographer saw at least two drones shot down near Erbil airport in northern Iraq. Two other devices were intercepted near the huge Saudi refinery of Ras Tanura, one of the largest in the world, which halted part of its operations, according to authorities. The war also reached European territory. According to the President of Cyprus, Nikos Christodoulides, an Iranian drone crashed into a British base on the Mediterranean island. Subsequently, a Cypriot government spokesperson stated that two drones, which were heading towards the British military base Akrotiri, were intercepted. In a joint statement, France, the United Kingdom and Germany threatened to adopt “defensive measures” to protect their interests and those of “allies in the region”, in addition to “destroying Iran's ability” to launch missiles and drones”.
Conflict can last weeks For now, there are no signs that the weapons can be silenced, with Israel warning that the operation in Iran will last “as long as necessary”. The country's army stated that attacks in Lebanon could continue for “many days”, but, at the moment, it sees no reason for an “invasion”. Israel has decided to mobilize 100,000 reservists for the offensive against Iran, and aviation will intensify its operations in Tehran, according to Netanyahu. In an interview with the “New York Times” (NYT), American President Donald Trump stated that the operation would last “four or five weeks” and predicted more casualties, in addition to the three American soldiers killed in an Iranian drone attack in Kuwait. The future of Iran is uncertain. Trump told the NYT that he has “three very good options” to govern Iran, but declined to reveal their names “at the moment”. Currently, the country is led by a triumvirate on a provisional basis, awaiting, according to Tehran, the election of Khamenei's successor. In a video message, Trump again urged the Revolutionary Guard and “military police” to “lay down their arms” in exchange for full immunity or otherwise face “certain death.” “We will not negotiate with the USA”, replied the head of the Iranian Supreme National Security Council, Ali Larijani, on the social network X. “Trump has plunged the region into chaos with his ‘illusory dreams’ and is now concerned about new casualties among American forces,” he added. Many Iranians celebrated Khamenei's death, but the announcement of the supreme leader's passing also sparked protests. Three days of attacks generated chaos in global air transport (with thousands of flight cancellations or delays) and in maritime traffic in the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Furthermore, the conflict caused oil and gas prices to soar. The price of a barrel of Brent crude rose almost 14%, while that of West Texas Intermediate rose 12% when markets opened after the attacks on Iran.
Beijing evacuates 3,000 citizens after Chinese man dies in Tehran China reported yesterday that more than 3,000 Chinese citizens have already been evacuated from Iran since the start of the military offensive in the country, after confirming the death of one of its nationals in Tehran due to the conflict. The Foreign Ministry spokeswoman said that since the security situation “became tense”, the Chinese ministry, embassy and consulates in Iran have issued several warnings and urged Chinese citizens to leave the country “as quickly as possible”. Mao Ning also reiterated the urgent call for a ceasefire and a return to diplomatic channels in the Middle East. “The most urgent task is to cease military operations to prevent the conflict from spreading,” said the spokeswoman, once again accusing the US and Israel of “violating international law”. According to Mao, “the Chinese side was not informed in advance” about the US military operation. Regarding Iranian warnings about maritime transit in the Persian Gulf, he declared that “the Strait of Hormuz and its surrounding waters are important international channels for the trade of goods and energy”. “Safeguarding security and stability in this region serves the common interests of the international community,” he added.
JTM with international agencies

item.title: Review of consumption tax aims to accelerate return on capital
item.description:
The Consumption Tax Regulation will be subject to review, with DSEDT collecting opinions until March 15. The idea is to further simplify procedures and reduce the time needed to return capital to companies, in the context of importing tobacco products and alcoholic beverages
The Directorate of Economics and Technological Development Services (DSEDT) launched a collection of opinions, which runs until March 15th, as part of the proposal to revise the Consumption Tax Regulation. With the review, the authorities intend to “optimize the current procedures for importing goods” – specifically drinks with an alcohol content greater than or equal to 30% and chopped meats, cigars and cigarettes -, “making it easier for the sector to carry out its activities and reducing the time required for the return of capital”, according to the consultation document. The main point is the “postponement of the tax payment period” from “the time when the import license is issued” to the “customs clearance phase”. DSEDT will make a settlement in accordance with the import license application and the amount of excise tax due will be withheld in the bank account designated by the operator. “After receiving the successful retention message sent by the bank, DSEDT will immediately issue the import license to the operator”, it can be read. During the customs clearance period, after confirmation by the Customs Services of the “real quantity” of imported goods subject to tax, the system will update the amount of tax due, which will be deducted from the bank account. If there is any remaining unused balance, it will be “released”. In this way, operators stop paying the tax when the import license is issued, that is, before the goods arrive in Macau, with the amount being retained, but in the operator's bank account. This reduces the time needed to return the capital, as they no longer have to submit a tax refund request and wait to receive the amount. To implement the change in the postponement of the tax payment period, some adaptations are necessary. When importing alcoholic beverages and tobacco products using the “Simultaneous Voluntary Payment Scheme”, operators only need to open an account with a participating bank, providing a single authorization instruction to allow DSEDT to withhold, release and deduct from the account the amount of excise tax due for each import license issuance. Importers of tobacco products and alcoholic beverages can now give their suggestions, with four sessions scheduled for the 9th and 10th of March to collect sectoral opinions. In addition, companies and residents who are interested in the subject can participate in the opinion gathering session that will take place at 3 pm on the 10th, and interested parties can register during office hours.
CP.

item.title: Public spending rose 4.4% last year
item.description:
Public expenditure increased by 4.4% in 2025, due to the reinforcement of social support, but the Macao SAR Administration ended the year with a surplus of almost 20 billion patacas. According to data published online by the Financial Services Directorate (DSF), Macau spent 98.41 billion patacas by the end of December, still 11.8% less than expected. The main reason for the increase compared to 2024 was spending on social support and subsidies, which grew by 6%, to 55.26 billion patacas. At the beginning of July, the Legislative Assembly of Macau approved a Government proposal to increase the expenses foreseen in the budget by 2.86 billion patacas, to reinforce social support. The review includes the creation of a subsidy, worth a total of MOP54,000, for children up to three years old, in an attempt to increase the lowest birth rate in the world. Expenses for civil servants also rose by 1.6%, to 16.51 billion patacas, despite civil servants not having any salary increase in 2025. At the end of November, the Macau Government announced that it will also not review civil servants' salaries in 2026, a decision justified by prudence and low inflation. According to official data, at the end of 2025 the region's public service had 33,856 workers, 325 fewer than in 2024. The statistics do not reveal how many have Portuguese nationality, mentioning only that 226 were born in Portugal. The increase in expenditure was balanced by Macau's current revenue, which rose 4.7% in 2025, to 114.64 billion patacas, 6.2% more than forecast by the Government. The main factor for the increase was an increase of 7.6%, to 94.85 billion patacas, in revenue from gambling taxes – which represent 82.7% of the total. The six gaming operators pay a direct tax of 35% on gaming revenue, with 2.4% going to the Macau Social Security Fund and urban and tourist development, and 1.6% going to the Macau Foundation for cultural, educational, scientific, academic and philanthropic purposes. Total gross revenues of Macau casinos reached 247.4 billion patacas last year, an increase of 9.1%. The Macao SAR Administration ended 2025 with a surplus in public accounts of 19.88 billion patacas, 26.1% more than in the previous year. The Government's initial forecast for the entire year 2025 pointed to a surplus of 6.83 billion patacas. However, the revised budget, approved by the Legislative Assembly at the beginning of July, predicted a surplus of only 191.1 million patacas.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: Security Forces create “Police Youth Group”
item.description:
The Higher Security Forces School founded the first edition of the “Police Star Youth Group”, a program that marks a new chapter in training work and aims to cultivate in young people “the feeling of love for the Fatherland and Macau”
The “Grupo Juvenil da Estrela Policia” is a new training program established by the Escola Superior das Forças de Segurança (ESFSM), aimed at more professional and comprehensive development. With the creation of the group, the authorities hope to cultivate in young people “a feeling of love for the Motherland and Macau”. The project also represents a new advance in the youth activities organized by the ESFSM, both in the number of training hours and in the programmatic contents, presenting new provisions on the basis of previous youth activities. The training process covers education about the national situation, leadership and planning training, police discipline training, social service, visits and exchanges, among other content, “which comprehensively raises the comprehensive skills of young people”, according to a statement from ESFSM. At the founding and oath-taking ceremony, the School's director, Wong Chi Fai, encouraged the young members to “dedicate themselves seriously to each training activity and practical project, cultivating a spirit of dedication to service, clarifying their responsibilities and missions, and becoming promising young people with ambition and a sense of responsibility”. Afterwards, armbands were assigned to the chief, deputy chief and members of the group, “symbolizing commitment to responsibility and mission”, states the note. After the ceremony, Zhao Guoqiang, professor at the Faculty of Law of the University of Macau, and Lai Chio Hong, head of the Security Department of the Judicial Police, gave a thematic lecture on “National Security”, explaining the importance of the Law relating to the defense of State security, the content of the “General Perspective on National Security” and the results achieved by Macau in this area. The members of the youth group then underwent police physical training under the guidance of the instructors. Through this series of training, “the spirit of discipline, unity and perseverance of the young members were reinforced”, highlights the text. The police school, which has been organizing education about the national situation and police discipline training, will also carry out other diverse actions, including education about the legal system, organization and planning of activities, team management, among others, “in order to raise the individual capabilities and integral qualities of young people in different aspects”.
V.R.

item.title: EPM won third place in football tournament in China
item.description:
The Portuguese School football team reached third place in the tournament that took place at the Zhuhai Sports Academy. EPM participated with category A, with players under 18 years of age, having finished the group stage in first place. The team captain, Miguel Rezende, told TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper that it was “five unforgettable days”
VÍTOR REBELO
The Portuguese School of Macau (EPM) caught the eye at the “Future Stars” International Youth Tournament, held at the Zhuhai Sports Academy, by finishing third on the podium, in a competition featuring 16 teams. Young people from EPM's A category, under 18, finished at the top of the group, after one victory and two draws with two Chinese teams and one South Korean. The difference in goals scored and conceded was decisive for qualification. In the qualifiers, the Macao SAR team was not present in the title discussion because they were defeated in the semi-finals by an academy from South Korea. Miguel Rezende, team captain, told TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper that they were “five unforgettable days”, during which the school football team “lived a unique experience in China”. It was “a remarkable journey, both on a social level, for socializing, and on a football level, where we learned and evolved a lot”, he added. The 18-year-old athlete, who, in addition to being part of the EPM squad, also plays in the Elite League, wearing the Chiba shirt, highlights that the team has improved its quality of play. Furthermore, he emphasizes, “this adventure strengthened our relationship between players, creating bonds that go far beyond the field”. As for support, in addition to the school director, Acácio de Brito, “the availability and dedication of the teachers who accompanied us was fundamental, making all this possible”, without compromising the normal functioning of the curricular plan, he stressed. Regarding the results, Miguel Rezende states that “thanks to our effort, unity and willpower we managed to achieve third place, a result that crowns this fantastic moment”. Meanwhile, one of the coaches, Luís Moura, highlighted that “it was an honorable place” for the school, after an “intense competition” against Chinese academies and elite teams from South Korea. “Participation was marked by the high level of competition and an experience considered unique for the young athletes”, he highlights. Throughout the tournament, continued the coach, who partnered with Arlindo Serro, “the team faced opponents with significantly better preparation, training five times a week and accompanied by technical teams made up of five to six members”. In contrast, EPM's A level participated with just 16 students, playing daily games lasting 90 minutes, “which made the challenge even more demanding”, observes the coach, for whom qualification in the group stage “was attributed to the strong team spirit, the determination shown on the field and the players' ability to sacrifice throughout the matches”. Aware of the technical and individual quality of their opponents, the team opted for a more cautious strategy in the knockout phase, “based on solid defensive organization”, an approach that, according to Luís Moura, “proved to be effective given the high pace and technical evolution of the Asian teams, with special emphasis on the South Korean teams, recognized for their effectiveness in individual duels”. The physical education teacher says that the team “maintained a positive attitude” throughout the competition, showing “resilience, a spirit of mutual help and a strong desire to learn”, characteristics that proved to be “determining for the performance achieved”. As a summary of the first participation in competitions across borders, the coach states that the delegation returned to Macau “with the conviction that the experience contributed not only to sporting growth, but also to the strengthening of the group as a team”, pointing out that “the passion for football and the desire to overcome were the main assets demonstrated throughout the competition”.

item.title: Imposed measures to reduce the impact of works on traffic
item.description: The Working Group for the Optimization of Road Works Coordination will promote this year, in the preparatory phase of road works, a gradual review of the number and distribution of existing manhole covers within the scope of the intended intervention. The Secretary for Transport and Public Works, Tam Vai Man, present at the Working Group meeting for the first quarter of 2026, gave instructions that, based on the 47 coordination zones integrated last year, the five guidelines should continue to be further developed. The objective is to adopt several measures “in conjunction with the needs of the population”, reducing the number of manholes, “in order to minimize, as much as possible, the impact of road works on traffic”, according to a statement from the Directorate of Services for Traffic Affairs. Under the condition that the technical requirements are met, the merger or elimination of these covers (essential elements to cover and protect inspection boxes, sewage, drainage and underground technical networks) will be carried out in order to reduce their quantity, improve road circulation conditions and guarantee the safety of motorcyclists. Furthermore, until February this year, through interdepartmental coordination, the Working Group coordinated more than 20 projects for joint execution of works, continuing to promote work on reserving connection pipes in undeveloped lots and the adoption of non-excavated techniques in carrying out works.

item.title: International visitors rose almost 16% in January
item.description: In January, Macau received 3.65 million visitors, a similar number to last year. The Statistics and Census Services Directorate highlights that the number of day trip arrivals (2,254,699) increased by 4.6% in annual terms, however, the number of tourists (1,392,629) decreased by 6.6%. Regarding the origin of visitors, arrivals from Mainland China stood at 2,705,231, down 1.6% in annual terms. The number of arrivals of visitors with individual visas (1,452,011) fell by 9.6%, “mainly due to the high basis of comparison, caused by the Lunar New Year holidays taking place in Mainland China at the end of January last year”. Visitors from Hong Kong also decreased, in this case by 1.3% to 570,490, while those from Taiwan rose by 20.7% to 93,154. In the same vein, the number of international visitor arrivals totaled 278,453 in January, an increase of 15.5% in annual terms. From Southeast Asia, those coming from Malaysia (18,252, up 52.3%), Thailand (18,039, up 80.5%) and Singapore (10,128, up 28.6%) stand out. 10,244 arrived from India, up 54.1% year on year, while 75,198 came from Korea (up 14.5%). In relation to long-distance visitors, the number of arrivals from the USA (12,948) increased by 4.7% year-on-year. On the other hand, the average occupancy rate of hotel rooms stood at 91.6%, registering a year-on-year increase of 0.7 percentage points. The number of visitors arriving on tours was 139,000, a drop of 13.4%.

item.title: Percussion and wind resonate Chinese folklore and modernity
item.description: Zhang Weiliang, a renowned performer of dizi, a traditional Chinese transverse flute made from bamboo, will join the Macau Chinese Orchestra, as artistic director, in the “Batida Respiração” concert, for “a skillful dialogue between the wind and percussion sections”, according to a press release from the Cultural Institute. The ensemble will be led by the Orchestra's musical director and main conductor, Zhang Lie, at 7:45 pm on March 6, in the Small Auditorium of the Macau Cultural Center. The Orchestra promises “a mix of resonant percussion and fluid winds, revealing the fascination of Chinese instrumental music”. The repertoire will include “traditional folk songs and modern pieces with new musical arrangements, including ‘Hanging the Red Lanterns’, ‘Lantern Festival’, ‘Despedida Festiva’, ‘Tears for the Flowers and Congratulations’”. The pieces “O Rio Amarelo Jorra do Céu” and “Ao Nascer do Sol”, a work written by Tian Ding, suona performer of the Chinese Orchestra, will also be played. The concert will highlight “the vibrant sound of wind instruments and the powerful resonance of percussion”. The note describes Zhang Weiliang as “a true master of stage performance, composition and musical education, as well as a pioneer of ‘new folk music’”. In fact, he performed “some of the soundtracks most cherished by the public of classic films”, such as “Adeus Minha Concubina”, “Ju Dou” and “Lanterna Vermelha”. Part of the Macau Chinese Orchestra Concert Season 2025/2026, the show will last around an hour and a half, with an intermission, and tickets cost between 150 and 200 patacas and can be purchased at the Macau Online Ticket Office or by telephone.

item.title: Virtual visits include four more heritage sites
item.description: The Cultural Institute (IC) launched online virtual reality (VR) guided tours for four more World Heritage sites, including the Protestant Cemetery, the Fundação Oriente Headquarters Building, the Maritime and Water Services Directorate Building (Former Quartel dos Moors) and the Guia Lighthouse (interior). The objective, according to the IC, is “to better integrate cultural resources, take advantage of digital technology to promote the value of Macau's World Heritage Site and contribute to boosting the development of Macau as a smart city”. So far, the IC has produced online VR guided tours for 15 World Heritage sites and eight squares, along with cultural facilities such as galleries, and online guided tour services are available for a total of 30 cultural sites and facilities. The public can enjoy the activity through the page with the same name. In addition to guided tours in Cantonese, Mandarin, Portuguese and English, this service also includes a 360º panoramic view, showing the buildings and the environment that surrounds them in detail. Users can even use their own virtual reality glasses to enhance the immersive experience. The IC promises to continue carrying out studies and developing online visits to World Heritage sites, “promoting the integration and development of cultural heritage resources and technologies, so as to provide diverse and convenient forms of cultural exploration, enriching the cultural heritage experience”.

item.title: Japanese students perform kabuki scene in English for international audience
item.description:
Japanese students from Linden Hall School in Chikushino, Fukuoka Prefecture, performed a kabuki scene in English at the opening ceremony of the World Federation of Tour Guides Associations (WFTGA) Fukuoka 2026 Convention on February 9. An important multicultural moment, very important for the Western dissemination of Japanese culture. The students performed under the guidance of kabuki actor Nakamura Kazutaro, who played a similar role in the hit film “Kokuho”. The audience, made up of around 500 people, included professional tour guides from 47 countries and regions, such as France, Germany, USA, Australia, Egypt, South Africa, Philippines and Singapore. The students realized that the traditional Japanese performance was greatly appreciated. English phrases like “(We) will not spare you!” or "Shut up! Shut up!" were used. The sound echoed across the stage, accompanied by the sounds of shamisen and taiko drums, in the opening ceremony. The association held its general assembly in Japan for the first time at the Fukuoka International Congress Center in the Hakata neighborhood of Fukuoka between 9 and 13 February. The 10 primary and secondary school students performed “Pulling the Carriage”, a scene from “The Secrets of Sugawara Calligraphy”. The play is based on the story of Sugawara no Michizane, who was exiled to Dazaifu, a regional government that existed in Fukuoka Prefecture during the Heian period (794-1185). The long speeches in English, delivered with the characteristic kabuki intonation, caused the audience to laugh frequently on the spot, indicating that the content seemed well understood by many. Classic kabuki screams were heard in Japanese, giving the audience a taste of a traditional performance. WFTGA president Sebastian Frankenberger, 44, from Germany, said it was his first time watching a kabuki show, but he found the battles and discussions in the play interesting. The play is easy to follow and very fun. The students' movements were perfectly synchronized and made a real impact, he said. “I felt that the audience noticed and laughed together,” said Yura Imasato, 16, a first-year high school student who played one of the main roles. "Translating the lines into English is challenging, but it makes kabuki more accessible. I want to continue studying kabuki." With its philosophy of “Japanese spirit with Western learning,” Linden High School graduates individuals capable of communicating Japanese culture to the world in English. The school launched the English Kabuki project in Spring 2024. Students practice at the Hakataza Theater in the Hakata neighborhood of Fukuoka during school hours as part of their “comprehensive studies”. From the beginning, Nakamura instructed them about body movement and “ma” – pauses used to express emotion. His first public performance in Fukuoka, in October 2024, caught the attention of someone linked to an association of Japanese tour guides, which eventually led to the event in February. The Kabuki performance in English was a great success. The film “Kokuho” became a huge box office success, surpassing 20 billion yen and becoming the highest-grossing live-action Japanese film of all time. This success also increased students' interest in Kabuki in English. Nakamura taught an intensive four-day course at the school in late January. On the day of the performance, he visited the place to participate in hayashi, the musical ensemble that normally accompanies kabuki performances. “We performed kabuki in Japanese even when we performed abroad,” Nakamura told the audience after the students’ performance. "Kabuki in English is a new project that is expanding the possibilities of the art. We started this project in the hope of, one day, performing abroad." Shinya Hamada, 31, a Japanese teacher at the school and involved in the project, said: "I would be very happy if more people abroad became interested in kabuki through the students' activities. "We want to continue our efforts and tackle different plays in the future."
JTM with international agencies
